root_text.an3.68:1.6: Imesaṁ, āvuso, tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ ko viseso ko adhippayāso kiṁ nānākaraṇan’ti?
root_text.an3.68:1.7: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, bhikkhave, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ kinti byākareyyāthā”ti?
root_text.an3.68:1.8: “Bhagavaṁmūlakā no, bhante, dhammā bhagavaṁnettikā bhagavaṁpaṭisaraṇā. Sādhu vata, bhante, bhagavantaṁyeva paṭibhātu etassa bhāsitassa attho. Bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī”ti.
root_text.an3.68:1.9: “Tena hi, bhikkhave, suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an3.68:1.10: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an3.68:2.6: imesaṁ, āvuso, tiṇṇaṁ dhammānaṁ ko viseso ko adhippayāso kiṁ nānākaraṇan’ti?
root_text.an3.68:2.7: Evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, bhikkhave, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha:
root_text.an3.68:2.8: ‘rāgo kho, āvuso, appasāvajjo dandhavirāgī, doso mahāsāvajjo khippavirāgī, moho mahāsāvajjo dandhavirāgī’ti.
root_text.an3.68:3.1: ‘Ko panāvuso, hetu ko paccayo yena anuppanno vā rāgo uppajjati uppanno vā rāgo bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattatī’ti?
root_text.an3.68:3.3: Tassa subhanimittaṁ ayoniso manasi karoto anuppanno vā rāgo uppajjati uppanno vā rāgo bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an3.68:3.4: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena anuppanno vā rāgo uppajjati uppanno vā rāgo bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattatī’ti.
root_text.an3.68:4.1: ‘Ko panāvuso, hetu ko paccayo yena anuppanno vā doso uppajjati uppanno vā doso bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattatī’ti?
root_text.an3.68:4.3: Tassa paṭighanimittaṁ ayoniso manasi karoto anuppanno vā doso uppajjati uppanno vā doso bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an3.68:4.4: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena anuppanno vā doso uppajjati uppanno vā doso bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattatī’ti.
root_text.an3.68:5.1: ‘Ko panāvuso, hetu ko paccayo yena anuppanno vā moho uppajjati uppanno vā moho bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattatī’ti?
root_text.an3.68:5.3: Tassa ayoniso manasi karoto anuppanno vā moho uppajjati uppanno vā moho bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an3.68:5.4: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena anuppanno vā moho uppajjati uppanno vā moho bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya saṁvattatī’ti.
root_text.an3.68:6.1: ‘Ko panāvuso, hetu ko paccayo yena anuppanno ceva rāgo nuppajjati uppanno ca rāgo pahīyatī’ti?
root_text.an3.68:6.3: Tassa asubhanimittaṁ yoniso manasi karoto anuppanno ceva rāgo nuppajjati uppanno ca rāgo pahīyati.
root_text.an3.68:6.4: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena anuppanno ceva rāgo nuppajjati uppanno ca rāgo pahīyatī’ti.
root_text.an3.68:7.1: ‘Ko panāvuso, hetu ko paccayo yena anuppanno ceva doso nuppajjati uppanno ca doso pahīyatī’ti?
root_text.an3.68:7.3: Tassa mettaṁ cetovimuttiṁ yoniso manasi karoto anuppanno ceva doso nuppajjati uppanno ca doso pahīyati.
root_text.an3.68:7.4: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena anuppanno ceva doso nuppajjati uppanno ca doso pahīyatī’ti.
root_text.an3.68:8.1: ‘Ko panāvuso, hetu ko paccayo yena anuppanno ceva moho nuppajjati uppanno ca moho pahīyatī’ti?
root_text.an3.68:8.3: Tassa yoniso manasi karoto anuppanno ceva moho nuppajjati uppanno ca moho pahīyati.
root_text.an3.68:8.4: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, hetu ayaṁ paccayo yena anuppanno vā moho nuppajjati uppanno ca moho pahīyatī’”ti.

translation_text.an3.68:1.1: “Mendicants, if wanderers of other religions were to ask:
translation_text.an3.68:1.8: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. He is our guide and our refuge. Sir, may the Buddha himself please clarify the meaning of this. The mendicants will listen and remember it.”
translation_text.an3.68:1.9: “Well then, mendicants, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an3.68:2.1: “Mendicants, if wanderers of other religions were to ask:
translation_text.an3.68:2.8: ‘Greed, reverends, is mildly blameworthy, but slow to fade away. Hate is very blameworthy, but quick to fade away. Delusion is very blameworthy, and slow to fade away.’
translation_text.an3.68:3.1: And if they ask: ‘What is the cause, what is the reason why greed arises, and once arisen it increases and grows?’
translation_text.an3.68:3.2: You should say: ‘The beautiful feature of things.
translation_text.an3.68:3.3: When you apply the mind irrationally to the beautiful feature of things, greed arises, and once arisen it increases and grows.
translation_text.an3.68:3.4: This is the cause, this is the reason why greed arises, and once arisen it increases and grows.’
translation_text.an3.68:4.1: And if they ask: ‘What is the cause, what is the reason why hate arises, and once arisen it increases and grows?’
translation_text.an3.68:4.2: You should say: ‘The feature of harshness.
translation_text.an3.68:4.3: When you apply the mind irrationally to the feature of harshness, hate arises, and once arisen it increases and grows.
translation_text.an3.68:4.4: This is the cause, this is the reason why hate arises, and once arisen it increases and grows.’
translation_text.an3.68:5.1: And if they ask: ‘What is the cause, what is the reason why delusion arises, and once arisen it increases and grows?’
translation_text.an3.68:5.2: You should say: ‘Irrational application of mind.
translation_text.an3.68:5.3: When you apply the mind irrationally, delusion arises, and once arisen it increases and grows.
translation_text.an3.68:5.4: This is the cause, this is the reason why delusion arises, and once arisen it increases and grows.’
translation_text.an3.68:6.1: And if they ask, ‘What is the cause, what is the reason why greed doesn’t arise, or if it’s already arisen it’s given up?’
translation_text.an3.68:6.2: You should say: ‘The ugly feature of things.
translation_text.an3.68:6.3: When you apply the mind rationally on the ugly feature of things, greed doesn’t arise, or if it’s already arisen it’s given up.
translation_text.an3.68:6.4: This is the cause, this is the reason why greed doesn’t arise, or if it’s already arisen it’s given up.’
translation_text.an3.68:7.1: And if they ask, ‘What is the cause, what is the reason why hate doesn’t arise, or if it’s already arisen it’s given up?’
translation_text.an3.68:7.2: You should say: ‘The heart’s release by love.’
translation_text.an3.68:7.3: When you apply the mind rationally on the heart’s release by love, hate doesn’t arise, or if it’s already arisen it’s given up.
translation_text.an3.68:7.4: This is the cause, this is the reason why hate doesn’t arise, or if it’s already arisen it’s given up.’
translation_text.an3.68:8.1: And if they ask, ‘What is the cause, what is the reason why delusion doesn’t arise, or if it’s already arisen it’s given up?’
translation_text.an3.68:8.2: You should say: ‘Rational application of mind.
translation_text.an3.68:8.3: When you apply the mind rationally, delusion doesn’t arise, or if it’s already arisen it’s given up.
translation_text.an3.68:8.4: This is the cause, this is the reason why delusion doesn’t arise, or if it’s already arisen it’s given up.’”
